author: jtraglia
content: <@884836500037066862> <@586161934425128960> Sorry guys. I was OOO all of last week. Yes, what you've described is accurate. I'm open to accepting same-epoch proposer preferences but I still think we should disallow it. These preferences are only necessary to receive builder bids for more MEV income. If a validator were to be offline until the epoch of their proposal, I think the node should just self-build and propose that. If we allow same-slot preferences, I might cause a bunch of re-orgs if an attacker repeatedly sends preferences just before their proposal slot.

author: jameshe5018
content: interested in nico's opinion, maybe when making the beacon api spec we just allow current epoch to go through without broadcasting setting local caches to replace /eth/v1/validator/prepare_beacon_proposer and next epoch  actually broadcasts it? if we don't want to allow it for current epoch?

author: nflaig
content:
> These preferences are only necessary to receive builder bids for more MEV income
I don't like this argument, we are moving towards making local building unfeasible so the trustless builder path should be as robust as possible

author: nflaig
content: can't we just add a gossip check which states that `proposal_slot` of the proposer preference needs to be before current slot?

author: bharath.vedartham
content: Note that, we will likely deprecate validator registrations in the builder-api too and require builders to listen to proposer preferences gossip topic for info like gas limit etc.

author: bharath.vedartham
content: i think this check makes sense to me. so that clients can rebroadcast the proposer preferences

author: potuz
content: Do we really want to deal with these edge cases? I mean c'mon if you're so unlucky you should build locally anyway in these cases

author: potuz
content: you will still get bids and you may like them anyway

author: potuz
content: I don't think we should change the protocol to deal with these severe edge cases that pose no risk to the chain at all

author: nflaig
content: I haven't looked at the implemention yet but this extra restriction to only allow them to be broadcast for next epoch makes things more complext, not simpler, maybe <@884836500037066862> can speak more to that

author: potuz
content: I mean if the change is to simply allow preferences to be broadcast for current epoch and next epoch it's absolutely fine.  We're not adding a larger DOS surface at all

author: potuz
content: But in general the theme of changing protocol to deal with these types of edge cases seems ridiculous to me

author: nflaig
content:
not per spec right now
>  If a validator does not broadcast a
> `SignedProposerPreferences` message, this implies that the validator will not
> accept any trustless bids for that slot.
and I think it's fine to keep this note

author: potuz
content: I think this is arbitrarily brittle: any network participant that hasn't seen the preference for any reason, will not be propagating bids that can help liveness

author: potuz
content: there's no reason to stop information from flowing

author: potuz
content: the proposer can always decide to drop them anyway

author: jameshe5018
content: I mean ideally we use this chance to get rid of eth/v1/validator/prepare_beacon_proposer, i proposed above if we want to avoid broadcasting if when we have a beacon api spec we allow it being called just for the purposes of local building if we hit that edge case?

author: nflaig
content: not against that, we can drop them at application layer instead of gossip easily

author: nflaig
content: we can but it seems simpler if we could have both local cache and gossip propagation to be more aligned

author: jameshe5018
content: I think these edge cases happen more regularly than maybe someone would initially think, we've gone through a lot of bugs and iterations on the validator registration and prepare beacon proposer endpoints and if we want to get rid of those 2 in favor of this one I just want those edge cases to be covered
